purpose:
Provides east–west traffic control within Experteq‑hosted data centre environments to prevent lateral movement between workloads.
Enables consistent microsegmentation policy, visibility, and enforcement to reduce breach impact and support APRA CPS 234 / CPS 230 security control expectations.
consumers: All Experteq, All customers with DC hosted worklaods, Experteq, Customers
currentStack:
Software‑defined microsegmentation platforms (DC‑first)
Shortlisted vendors: Zero Networks, ColorTokens, Akamai Guardicore, Illumio
Integration with VMware/Hyper‑V estates and core firewall platforms
Centralised policy management with agent‑based or agentless enforcement (vendor‑dependent)
constraints:
Vendor cost models and commercial alignment as a managed service
Dependency on clear service design (table‑stakes vs premium tiers) before scale rollout
targetState:
A standardised, centrally managed microsegmentation service delivered across Experteq data centre environments.
Provides workload‑level east–west traffic control using software‑defined policy, enabling consistent enforcement, rapid isolation, and reduced blast radius without reliance on legacy network segmentation alone.
Delivered as a default (“table‑stakes”) security capability for managed customers, with optional advanced policy tiers over time.
targetDrivers:
Reduce impact and spread of security incidents within DC environments
Meet and demonstrate APRA CPS 234 / CPS 230 control intent
Customer expectation for modern zero‑trust–aligned security foundations
Improve automation and orchestration for both service delivery and ongoing operational support, reducing manual policy and rule management
Simplify and automate compliance evidence collection and reporting for both Experteq and customer regulatory and audit requirements

risks.description: Lateral movement risk within DC workloads (Customer raised)
Regulatory scrutiny on east–west traffic controls (CPS 234 / CPS 230)

actions.notes: Zero Networks, ColorTokens, Akamai Guardicore, Illumio
